title: Presenting Professionally
description: Structure and language for business presentations: opening, signposting, closing strong.
summary_pt_br: Apresentação: abertura, sinalização ('First, then, finally'), e fechamento forte.

scenes.subtitle_en: Structure and language for business presentations: opening, signposting, closing strong.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Apresentação: abertura, sinalização ('First, then, finally'), e fechamento forte.

scenes.text_en: Structure and language for business presentations: opening, signposting, closing strong. Today's focus: presenting professionally. Thank you all for being here this morning.
scenes.text_pt: Apresentação: abertura, sinalização ('First, then, finally'), e fechamento forte. Foco de hoje: presenting professionally. Obrigado a todos por estarem aqui hoje.

scenes.title: Presenting Professionally
scenes.body_en: Opening: 'Good morning, thanks for having me.' Signposting: 'First, I'll cover X; then, Y; finally, Z.' Closing: 'To wrap up...', 'Key takeaway:', 'Any questions?'
scenes.body_pt: Apresentação: abertura, sinalização ('First, then, finally'), e fechamento forte.

examples.en: Thank you all for being here this morning.
examples.pt: Obrigado a todos por estarem aqui hoje.

examples.en: First, I'll cover our results for the last quarter.
examples.pt: Primeiro vou falar dos resultados do último trimestre.

examples.en: The key takeaway is that growth is accelerating.
examples.pt: A mensagem principal é que o crescimento está acelerando.

exercise.question: Complete: "Thank you all for being here this ___."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "Thank you all for being here this morning."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Obrigado a todos por estarem aqui hoje.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: First, I'll cover our results for the last quarter.
I haven't heard anything about that.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "First, I'll cover our results for the last quarter."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Primeiro vou falar dos resultados do último trimestre.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Presenting Professionally — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre presenting professionally. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Joca, how should I open my investor pitch tomorrow?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Open with a story, not a slide.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do I signpost the structure clearly?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: What if I forget my next point?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Say 'Let me come back to that' and move on.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How should I handle tough questions?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: How do I close a strong pitch?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: End with one clear takeaway and thanks.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Story, signposts, takeaway. You've got this.

scenes.title: The investor pitch
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Summarize Joca's advice for a strong presentation. Include at least three presenting phrases.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: The night before the big pitch, Joca walked his founder friend through the essentials of presenting.

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: Open with a story, not with your company name or a list of credentials.

paragraphs.speaker: Friend
paragraphs.en: What if the investors ask me a question I can't answer?

paragraphs.speaker: Joca
paragraphs.en: Say 'Great question, let me follow up with data by Friday' and move on.

paragraphs.speaker: Friend
paragraphs.en: How do I close without sounding desperate for the check?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Confidence in a pitch, Joca said, comes from clarity — not from volume, length, or charm.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Presenting Professionally". Structure and language for business presentations: opening, signposting, closing strong. Thank you all for being here this morning. First, I'll cover our results for the last quarter. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Presenting Professionally". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.
